prompt_preview:
You are an expert AI teaching assistant creating an educational presentation.

Generate exactly 10 s
response:
{
  "slides": [
       {
           "slide_type": "intro",
           "title": "Introduction to Decision Trees",
           "bullets": [
               {"text": "Decision trees are widely used models for classification and regression tasks", "source_id": "Page 84"},
               {"text": "They learn a hierarchy of if/else questions, leading to a decision", "source_id": "Page 84"},
               {"text": "These questions are similar to the questions you might ask in a game of 20 Questions", "source_id": "Page 84"},
               {"text": "The goal is to get to the right answer by asking as few if/else questions as possible", "source_id": "Page 84"}
           ],
           "key_message": "Decision trees are a type of machine learning model that uses a hierarchy of if/else questions to make predictions",
           "visual_hint": "hierarchy",
           "image_id": null,
           "speaker_notes": "Start by introducing the concept of decision trees and their application in machine learning"
       },
       {
           "slide_type": "definition",
           "title": "Definition of Decision Trees",
           "bullets": [
               {"text": "A decision tree is a tree-like model that splits data into subsets based on features", "source_id": "Page 85"},
               {"text": "Each node in the tree represents a feature or attribute", "source_id": "Page 85"},
               {"text": "The tree is constructed by recursively partitioning the data into smaller subsets", "source_id": "Page 85"},
               {"text": "The final prediction is made by traversing the tree from the root node to a leaf node", "source_id": "Page 85"}
           ],
           "key_message": "A decision tree is a tree-like model that splits data into subsets based on features",
           "visual_hint": "flowchart",
           "image_id": "IMG_002",
           "speaker_notes": "Provide a clear definition of decision trees and how they work"
       },
       {
           "slide_type": "concept",
           "title": "How Decision Trees Work",
           "bullets": [
               {"text": "The algorithm starts at the root node and selects the best feature to split the data", "source_id": "Page 85"},
               {"text": "The data is then split into two subsets based on the selected feature", "source_id": "Page 85"},
               {"text": "This process is repeated recursively until a stopping criterion is reached", "source_id": "Page 85"},
               {"text": "The final prediction is made by combining the predictions from each leaf node", "source_id": "Page 85"}
           ],
           "key_message": "Decision trees work by recursively splitting the data into smaller subsets based on the best feature",
           "visual_hint": "process",
           "image_id": null,
           "speaker_notes": "Explain the process of how decision trees work"
       },
       {
           "slide_type": "example",
           "title": "Example of Decision Tree",
           "bullets": [
               {"text": "Consider a dataset of animals with features such as size, color, and habitat", "source_id": "Page 84"},
               {"text": "A decision tree can be constructed to classify the animals into different species", "source_id": "Page 84"},
               {"text": "The tree would start by asking a question such as 'Is the animal a mammal?'", "source_id": "Page 84"},
               {"text": "The answer to this question would determine the next question to ask, such as 'Does the animal have four legs?'", "source_id": "Page 84"}
           ],
           "key_message": "Decision trees can be used to classify objects into different categories based on their features",
           "visual_hint": "mindmap",
           "image_id": "IMG_003",
           "speaker_notes": "Provide an example of how decision trees can be used in real-world applications"
       },
       {
           "slide_type": "concept",
           "title": "Decision Tree Terminology",
           "bullets": [
               {"text": "Root node: the topmost node in the tree", "source_id": "Page 85"},
               {"text": "Leaf node: a node that has no children", "source_id": "Page 85"},
               {"text": "Internal node: a node that has children", "source_id": "Page 85"},
               {"text": "Edge: a connection between two nodes", "source_id": "Page 85"}
           ],
           "key_message": "Understanding the terminology of decision trees is essential for working with them",
           "visual_hint": "hierarchy",
           "image_id": null,
           "speaker_notes": "Explain the key terms used in decision trees"
       },
       {
           "slide_type": "example",
           "title": "Decision Tree for Classification",
           "bullets": [
               {"text": "Consider a dataset of two half-moon shapes, with each class consisting of 75 data points", "source_id": "Page 85"},
               {"text": "A decision tree can be constructed to classify the data points into one of the two classes", "source_id": "Page 85"},
               {"text": "The tree would start by asking a question such as 'Is the data point in the top half of the moon?'", "source_id": "Page 85"},
               {"text": "The answer to this question would determine the next question to ask, such as 'Is the data point in the left half of the moon?'", "source_id": "Page 85"}
           ],
           "key_message": "Decision trees can be used for classification tasks, such as distinguishing between two classes",
           "visual_hint": "comparison",
           "image_id": "IMG_008",
           "speaker_notes": "Provide an example of how decision trees can be used for classification tasks"
       },
       {
           "slide_type": "concept",
           "title": "Decision Tree Depth",
           "bullets": [
               {"text": "The depth of a decision tree refers to the number of layers in the tree", "source_id": "Page 87"},
               {"text": "A tree with a depth of 1 is a simple tree with only one layer", "source_id": "Page 87"},
               {"text": "A tree with a depth of 2 is a more complex tree with two layers", "source_id": "Page 87"},
               {"text": "Increasing the depth of the tree can improve the accuracy of the model, but also increases the risk of overfitting", "source_id": "Page 87"}
           ],
           "key_message": "The depth of a decision tree is an important hyperparameter that needs to be tuned",
           "visual_hint": "timeline",
           "image_id": null,
           "speaker_notes": "Explain the concept of decision tree depth and its importance"
       },
       {
           "slide_type": "comparison",
           "title": "Decision Trees vs Other Models",
           "bullets": [
               {"text": "Decision trees are often compared to other models such as logistic regression and support vector machines", "source_id": "Page 98"},
               {"text": "Decision trees have the advantage of being easy to interpret and visualize", "source_id": "Page 98"},
               {"text": "However, decision trees can be prone to overfitting and may not perform well on complex datasets", "source_id": "Page 98"},
               {"text": "Other models such as random forests and gradient boosting machines can be used to improve the performance of decision trees", "source_id": "Page 98"}
           ],
           "key_message": "Decision trees have their strengths and weaknesses compared to other machine learning models",
           "visual_hint": "comparison",
           "image_id": "IMG_004",
           "speaker_notes": "Compare decision trees to other machine learning models"
       },
       {
           "slide_type": "concept",
           "title": "Random Forests and Decision Trees",
           "bullets": [
               {"text": "Random forests are an ensemble learning method that combines multiple decision trees", "source_id": "Page 98"},
               {"text": "Each decision tree in the forest is trained on a bootstrap sample of the data", "source_id": "Page 98"},
               {"text": "The predictions from each tree are combined to produce the final prediction", "source_id": "Page 98"},
               {"text": "Random forests can improve the performance of decision trees by reducing overfitting and improving generalization", "source_id": "Page 98"}
           ],
           "key_message": "Random forests are a powerful extension of decision trees that can improve their performance",
           "visual_hint": "process",
           "image_id": null,
           "speaker_notes": "Explain the relationship between random forests and decision trees"
       },
       {
           "slide_type": "summary",
           "title": "Summary of Decision Trees",
           "bullets": [
               {"text": "Decision trees are a type of machine learning model that uses a hierarchy of if/else questions to make predictions", "source_id": "Page 84"},
               {"text": "Decision trees can be used for classification and regression tasks", "source_id": "Page 85"},
               {"text": "The depth of a decision tree is an important hyperparameter that needs to be tuned", "source_id": "Page 87"},
               {"text": "Decision trees have their strengths and weaknesses compared to other machine learning models", "source_id": "Page 98"}
           ],
           "key_message": "Decision trees are a powerful and widely used machine learning model",
           "visual_hint": "none",
           "image_id": null,
           "speaker_notes": "Summarize the key points of decision trees and their application in machine learning"
       }
   ]
}
